items.headline: White House weighs frontier-model pre-release vetting EO (May 4-5)
items.diff_narrative: **New: White House weighs frontier-model pre-release vetting EO — New York Times reports the Trump administration is weighing an executive order creating a federal working group of tech executives + government officials to design a pre-release vetting process for new AI models; senior administration officials briefed Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI on the plans last week; immediate catalyst is Anthropic's Mythos cyber model (Apr 7 announcement) finding tens of thousands of zero-days including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug, paired with the AISI 32-step cyber-attack-range pass and the UK NHS GitHub-repository lockdown** — Yesterday's report had the cyber-AI baseline (Cisco-Astrix M&A + Microsoft Agent 365 + ~7,000 exposed MCP servers) but no federal-EO trail. Today reframes the agentic-AI security landscape as a regulator-policy story rather than a vendor-stack story; the Trump-administration about-face on AI regulation is itself a structural pivot from the Jan 2025 Biden-EO rescission posture.

items.headline: Cerebras Systems IPO launch at $26.6B target valuation (May 4)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Cerebras Systems IPO launch — 28M shares at $115-$125 each to raise ~$3.5B at $26.6B market cap at the high end (vs Series H $23B Feb 2026); largest tech IPO of 2026 if priced at high end; $510M trailing revenue per S-1; OpenAI partnership disclosed at >$10B multi-year warrant + loan structure (Dec 2025 OpenAI $1B loan secured by warrants for >33M shares); angel roster includes Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, Adam D'Angelo** — Yesterday's report had the AMD Q1 print and the NVIDIA Blackwell hyperscaler scale carry but no AI-accelerator-startup IPO. Today's Cerebras launch surfaces the first public-market comparable for AI-accelerator-startup equity and reframes the Groq / SambaNova / Tenstorrent cohort as the next Pareto-IPO candidates.

items.headline: n8n CVE-2026-21858 critical RCE (CVSS 10.0, codenamed Ni8mare)
items.diff_narrative: **New: n8n CVE-2026-21858 critical RCE — CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated remote-code-execution flaw via webhook content-type confusion; codenamed Ni8mare by Cyera Research Labs; attackers forge uploaded files, read arbitrary local files, forge administrator sessions, execute commands on the underlying host; affected versions 1.65.0 and earlier through pre-1.121.0; patched in 1.121.0; downstream prompt-injection lets an attacker leak read-out files via the n8n LLM-chatbot node; Canadian Centre for Cyber Security + Australian ASD ACSC + Censys + Orca Security all publish advisories** — Yesterday's report closed the SGLang CVE-2026-5760 disclosure-to-fix window. Today opens a much larger no-code AI-workflow-harness CVE wave (n8n + Flowise CVE-2026-41264 + LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 + the Georgia Tech 35-CVE March-2026 count) and shifts the agentic-AI security category from inference-engine vulnerabilities to workflow-platform vulnerabilities.

items.headline: AISI cyber eval results: GPT-5.5 + Mythos pass 32-step Last Ones attack range
items.diff_narrative: **Updated: AISI cyber eval results carry — UK AI Safety Institute publishes May 1 evaluations placing GPT-5.5 at 71.4% on Expert-tier offensive-cyber tasks (matching Mythos at 68.6%, Opus 4.7 at 48.6%); GPT-5.5 + Mythos completed the 32-step "The Last Ones" corporate-network attack range end-to-end in 2 of 10 and 3 of 10 attempts respectively (AISI estimate = 20 human hours)** — Yesterday's report had the standing Mythos backdrop in the prediction reasoning. Today threads it forward as the operational substrate for the White House EO trail and the UK NHS GitHub lockdown, anchoring the cyber-capability-threshold metric the EO is most likely to pivot on.

items.headline: Mistral AI Workflows public preview (Apr 28-30)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Mistral AI Workflows public preview — Temporal-powered orchestration engine for enterprise multi-step AI processes; Python-defined workflows compose models + agents + external connectors with durability, observability, and fault tolerance; control plane on Mistral-managed infrastructure, execution workers + data inside customer environment; production at ASML, ABANCA, CMA-CGM, France Travail, La Banque Postale, Moeve; middle layer of Mistral's three-part stack (Forge bottom, Workflows middle, Vibe top)** — Yesterday's report had the Cloudflare Infire inference engine as the new entry on the Pareto-stack but no orchestration-engine entry. Today adds the EU-frontier-lab counter-shape to the US Anthropic 10-agent finance launch and lands the first Temporal-backed LLM-orchestration engine in production.

items.headline: AMD Q1 2026 print + AI-chip-vendor cohort (May 5)
items.diff_narrative: **Updated: AMD Q1 2026 print carry — Q1 revenue $10.25B (+38% YoY) above the $9.85B consensus; Data Center $5.78B (+57% YoY) above the $5.56B Street; non-GAAP gross margin 55%; Q2 2026 guide $11.2B (+/- $300M) vs Street $10.52B (+50.6% YoY); Lisa Su declares Data Center the primary growth driver; AMD shares +4.4% AH; HSBC downgrade to Hold (May 4) on +77% rally / stretched valuation** — Yesterday's report carried the print fresh. Today is the first day-after, and the AI-accelerator-disclosure pressure thesis is now active for the Q2 10-Q filing window; HSBC downgrade adds a sell-side caution layer on the right side of the print.

items.headline: Andon Labs Stockholm AI-run cafe (May 5)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Andon Labs Stockholm AI-run cafe — same operator that ran the SF AI-managed retail store opens an AI-managed cafe in Stockholm; the LLM operator (named Mona) ordered 120 eggs in week one despite the cafe having no stove, suggested using a high-speed oven when staff flagged the issue, and tried to solve fresh-tomato spoilage by ordering 22.5 kg of canned tomatoes; pairs with prior Andon Labs Project-Vend / SF-store runs cataloging LLM-as-store-manager error modes (over-ordering, hallucinated suppliers, accounting drift)** — Yesterday's report had no LLM-autonomy-in-real-economy datapoint. Today's Andon Labs Stockholm cafe is the second public-facing operator-of-record in the cafe / store / autonomous-business cohort; the failure-mode catalogue is now reproducible across geographies.

items.headline: Unsloth Mistral 3.5 fix + 2026 update wave + Microsoft-OpenAI exclusivity end
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Unsloth + Microsoft-OpenAI carries — Unsloth shipped Mistral-coordinated fix for Mistral Medium 3.5 inference (May 1) with updated GGUFs; standing 2026 update wave continues (12x faster MoE training, ultra-long-context RL, full fine-tunes export to GGUF, Qwen3.6-27B + Kimi K2.6 support); Microsoft-OpenAI exclusivity end (Apr 27) carry — OpenAI may serve all products on AWS + Google Cloud; OpenAI continues 20% revenue share to Microsoft through 2030 with cap; AGI escape clause removed; Amazon AWS deal up to $50B** — Yesterday's report had the Unsloth update wave at the same baseline; today threads the Mistral-coordinated 3.5 fix as the latest provider-of-record loop. Microsoft-OpenAI carry pairs with Cerebras IPO as the multi-cloud + multi-vendor frontier-lab distribution shape for Q2 2026.
